title: Red Sox’ Willson Contreras apologizes for recent on-field confrontations: ‘It’s been a really tough week’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Willson Contreras of the Boston Red Sox is appealing a seven-game suspension after a brawl with the Nationals.
Contreras's emotional on-field behavior stems from two devastating earthquakes in Venezuela, his home country.
Contreras and Nationals' Cade Cavalli both apologized for their roles in the recent altercation.

title: Red Sox Aroldis Chapman and Ranger Suarez selected to MLB All-Star Game
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox pitchers Aroldis Chapman and Ranger Suarez selected for the 2026 MLB All-Star Game in Philadelphia.
Chapman, dominant as a closer, has a 2.10 ERA and set a record for career strikeouts among relievers.
Suarez, leading a young pitching staff, has a 2.94 ERA through 16 starts, with 92 strikeouts in 88.2 innings.

title: Paul Pierce encourages Jaylen Brown after trade from Celtics: ‘This is what keeps you hungry’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Paul Pierce offered support to Jaylen Brown after the Celtics traded him to the 76ers, emphasizing growth through adversity.
Brown expressed feeling disrespected by the Celtics, citing past snubs like being left off the 2024 Olympic team.
Pierce expressed disappointment but was not surprised by the trade, optimistic about Celtics' future with Paul George.

title: Photos: Boston celebrates the Fourth of July
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Boston celebrated the 250th anniversary of Independence with a parade and Declaration reading.
Festivities included a parade featuring historical reenactments and performances by groups like the Wilmington Minutemen.
The Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular, with a drone show, was held at Hatch Memorial Shell.

title: Is Caleb Durbin’s turnaround enough to solidify his future with the Red Sox?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Caleb Durbin leads the Red Sox in OPS at .977 over the last 30 days, with 7 home runs.
Durbin's performance improved after working with a new hitting coach, taking over third base after contract issues with Alex Bregman.
Boston controls Durbin's contract until 2031, allowing roster flexibility while potentially easing salary cap for future acquisitions.

title: Red Sox’ Aroldis Chapman breaks MLB relief pitcher strikeout record in save vs. Angels
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Aroldis Chapman broke the MLB relief pitcher strikeout record with his 1,364th strikeout against the Angels.
Chapman achieved the milestone in 847 innings, surpassing Hoyt Wilhelm's previous record set in 1,872.1 innings.
The Red Sox celebrated Chapman's achievement with a video montage; the team may trade him by the August 3 deadline.

title: Allstate names the most collision-prone cities in the U.S.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston ranks as the most collision-prone city in the 2026 Allstate report with drivers averaging 3.7 years between crashes.
Worcester and Springfield, Massachusetts, rank among the bottom in Allstate's list, with average spans of 5.14 and 5.18 years respectively.
Brownsville, Texas, named safest city for drivers, with nearly 15 years average between collisions according to Allstate's annual report.

title: Falmouth man, 74, arrested after police say he led troopers on wrong-way, high-speed chase over Bourne Bridge
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Armando Deleon, 74, led police on a high-speed wrong-way chase across the Bourne Bridge reaching speeds up to 105 mph.
Deleon was charged with over a dozen offenses, including driving with a suspended license and assault with a dangerous weapon.
The pursuit began after Deleon nearly struck personnel during a medical emergency on Route 25; he was arrested after barricading himself at home.

title: Boston police release new photos of alleged lemonade stand robber
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston police released surveillance photos of a second juvenile suspect in the lemonade stand robbery on West Ninth Street.
A 14-year-old was previously arrested for two counts of armed robbery and unlawful possession of a firearm in the case.
Witnesses are urged to contact Boston Police with information on the robbery or the unidentified suspect's identity.

title: Jayson Tatum and Brad Stevens appear on Rich Paul’s big board of LeBron James landing spots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: LeBron James is considering his final NBA team and has informed the Lakers of his departure after eight seasons.
Rich Paul mentions Boston Celtics with Brad Stevens and Jayson Tatum as a potential landing spot for James.
Boston has bolstered its roster with Mitchell Robinson, Mike Conley Jr., and Paul George in recent offseason moves.
